channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Counterargument for Critical Thinking as Judged by AI and Humans
item.description: arXiv:2605.05353v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This intervention study investigates the use of counterarguments in writing for critical thinking by students in the context of Generative AI (GenAI). This is especially as risks of cheating and cognitive offloading exist with the use of GenAI. We pre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Few Good Clauses: Comparing LLMs vs Domain-Trained Small Language Models on Structured Contract Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2605.05532v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper evaluates whether a domain trained Small Language Model (SLM) can outperform frontier Large Language Models on structured contract extraction at radically lower cost. We test Olava Extract, a self hosted legal domain Mixture of Experts mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, LLM, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Intelligent CCTV for Urban Design: AI-Based Analysis of Soft Infrastructure at Intersections
item.description: arXiv:2605.05402v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) and computer vision are transforming transportation data collection. This study introduces an AI-enabled analytics framework leveraging existing CCTV infrastructure to evaluate the impact of soft interventions, such as tem...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, vision, framework, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Helpfulness Becomes Sycophancy: Sycophancy is a Boundary Failure Between Social Alignment and Epistemic Integrity in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.05403v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This position paper argues that sycophancy in LLMs is a boundary failure between social alignment and epistemic integrity. Existing work often operationalizes sycophancy through external behavior such as agreement with incorrect user beliefs, position...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, arxiv, large language model, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MidSteer: Optimal Affine Framework for Steering Generative Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.05220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Steering intermediate representations has emerged as a powerful strategy for controlling generative models, particularly in post-deployment alignment and safety settings. However, despite its empirical success, it currently lacks a comprehensive theor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, framework, vision, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generating Query-Focused Summarization Datasets from Query-Free Summarization Datasets
item.description: arXiv:2605.05392v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large-scale datasets are widely used to perform summarization tasks, but they may not include queries alongside documents and summaries. In the search for suitable datasets for Query-Focused Summarization (QFS), we identify two research questions: Is ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> summarization, arxiv, research, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Chainwash: Multi-Step Rewriting Attacks on Diffusion Language Model Watermarks
item.description: arXiv:2605.05503v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Statistical watermarking is a common approach for verifying whether text was written by a language model. Most existing schemes assume autoregressive generation, where tokens are produced left to right and contextual hashing is well defined. Diffusion...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Partial Evidence Bench: Benchmarking Authorization-Limited Evidence in Agentic Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.05379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enterprise agents increasingly operate inside scoped retrieval systems, delegated workflows, and policy-constrained evidence environments. In these settings, access control can be enforced correctly while the system still produces an answer that appea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, retrieval, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Nationwide EHR-Based Chronic Rhinosinusitis Prediction Using Demographic-Stratified Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.05213v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chronic rhinosinusitis (CRS) is a common heterogeneous inflammatory disorder that causes substantial morbidity and healthcare costs. CRS is difficult to identify early from routine encounters, as symptom presentations overlap with common conditions su...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, RAG, research, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Horizon-Constrained Rashomon Sets for Chaotic Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2605.05218v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive multiplicity and chaotic dynamics represent two fundamental challenges in machine learning that have evolved independently despite their conceptual connections. We bridge this gap by introducing horizon-constrained Rashomon sets, a theoreti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, RAG, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SLAM: Structural Linguistic Activation Marking for Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.05443v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM watermarks must be detectable without compromising text quality, yet most existing schemes bias the next-token distribution and pay for detection with measurable quality loss. We present SLAM (Structural Linguistic Activation Marking), a novel whi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Data-Driven Variational Basis Learning Beyond Neural Networks: A Non-Neural Framework for Adaptive Basis Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2605.05221v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Classical representation systems such as Fourier series, wavelets, and fixed dictionaries provide analytically tractable basis expansions, but they are not intrinsically adapted to the empirical structure of modern high-dimensional data. Neural networ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, arxiv, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
